title: Add evolution log
reflection: Created the evolution log system and daily-evolve workflow for autonomous improvement.
looking_ahead: Make temperament visible through color
Sound envelopes for bond formation/breaking
Event permanence: meteor craters, flood channels

title: Temperament colors
reflection: Replaced single energy-colored pixel with continuous hue from temperament axes.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for musical bond moments
Activate spatial hash grid for performance
Event permanence: terrain marks that persist through the cycle

title: Blended bond colors
reflection: Bond lines now blend the temperament colors of both endpoints. Pre-computed mote colors into a Map.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for bond formation chimes
Activate spatial hash grid
Event permanence: crater tiles, waterlogged terrain

title: Visible motes and bonds
reflection: Motes grew from 1px to 5px crosses, bonds visible at alpha 100, spatial hash grid wired up.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for bond formation
Death particles on mote expiration
Settlement markers with structural shapes

title: Phase-tinted sky
reflection: Sky now interpolates per-phase color tints: violet at genesis, amber at dissolution, indigo at silence.
looking_ahead: Death particles for dissolution visibility
Sound envelopes for bond chimes
Mote age visibility: gold tinge for elders

title: Visual overhaul
reflection: Added breathing pulse, cluster glow, soul-rise death particles, eclipse stars, aurora curtains, narrative text.
looking_ahead: WebGL post-processing for real bloom/glow
Creature memory: motes returning to meaningful places
Visible trails and history accumulating through the cycle
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Architecture refactor
reflection: Split 922-line main.ts into 25 focused files with clean layered DAG. No circular deps.
looking_ahead: WebGL post-processing for bloom/glow
Creature memory and meaningful return behaviors
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Death Made Dramatic
reflection: Rewrote death particles into four phases (flash, shards, spirit orb, ground echo lasting 7.5s) using the mote's actual temperament color instead of biome palette.
looking_ahead: Temperament movement distinction: wanderers leave longer trails, social motes pulse when bonded
Bond formation visual moment: a brief arc of light connecting two motes
Cluster identity: large clusters develop a shared color aura
Hardy motes: visual resilience cue on hostile terrain

title: Five Worlds, Five Voices
reflection: Each biome now has a distinct sonic identity: root pitch, reverb, filter Q, scale per phase. Added stereo panning, LFO filter breathing, fifth-interval bond chimes, and descending death glides.
looking_ahead: Spawn sound: gentle arrival tone per mote
Phase transition musical moment
Cluster fragmentation sound
Biome ambient texture beds

title: The Sky Learns to Tell Time
reflection: Added cycle-driven solar arc, biome-aware weather particles, biome fog identity, multi-frequency water shimmer, and depth-blended background mountains.
looking_ahead: Horizon glow at sky-terrain boundary
Desert heat shimmer distortion
Volcanic lava pools as terrain feature
Phase-reactive terrain during events

title: The Cycle Learns to Tell Its Own Story
reflection: Rewrote narrative arc with deterministic per-phase openings, event anticipation windows, aftermath lines, and expanded cycle name vocabulary.
looking_ahead: Bond formation flash between motes
Dissolution dirge for first death
Event cascade narrative for phase overlaps
Cluster identity names in narrative text

title: Cinematic Post-Processing
reflection: Added phase-specific color grading, rebuilt aurora with 5 overlapping layers, phase flash, phase-varying vignette, and warm glowing cursor.
looking_ahead: Bond formation arc streak
WebGL overlay for real bloom/glow
Phase-specific particle showers
Terrain color overlays per biome

title: Ambient Beds, Phase Chords, Spawn Pings
reflection: Added biome ambient beds, phase transition chord signatures, spread cluster voices across scale degrees, and spawn pings.
looking_ahead: Phase-reactive ambient bed volume
Cluster fragmentation sound
Bond-break downward glide
Event-ambient crossfade

title: Bond Arc, Wanderer Trails, Social Pulse
reflection: Wanderers leave brighter trails, bonded social motes show pulsing resonance rings, bond formation gets converging spark arc.
looking_ahead: Hardy mote terrain resilience flash
Wanderer sprite tilt toward movement
Cluster identity halo for 4+ groups
Bond break visual scatter

title: Biomes Made Real
reflection: Desert gets cacti instead of trees, volcanic gets rock spires, tundra gets dead trees. Volcanic water renders as animated lava. Added horizon glow band and tundra frost crystals.
looking_ahead: Desert heat shimmer distortion
Phase-reactive water level during flood
Volcanic ash accumulation overlay
Lush bloom event flower multiplication

title: Five New Emotional Beats
reflection: Added first-death dirge, peak population acknowledgment, last-survivor naming, and event-phase cascade lines with per-cycle variants.
looking_ahead: Cluster identity persistence with names
Silent-world final reflection line
Biome-aware dissolution text
Weather-event overlap narrative

title: Bond Break, Cluster Territory
reflection: Bond breaks scatter colored shards. Clusters of 4+ pulse a dashed perimeter ring; 6+ gain centroid spokes.
looking_ahead: Hardy mote terrain resilience flash
Wanderer sprite tilt
Bond break midpoint flash
Cluster fragmentation cascade

title: Screen-Space Bloom — Creatures as Light
reflection: Full screen-space bloom pass: threshold bright pixels, separable box blur, additive blend. Bloom strength varies by phase and spikes during eclipse. The world feels lit from within.
looking_ahead: WebGL compositing for real-time HDR tone-map and motion blur
Per-biome bloom tint (warm for volcanic, cool for tundra)
Interaction force-field visualization around cursor
Phase-specific particle showers (genesis stardust, dissolution ash)

title: Bond Break, Breathing Beds, Spatial Voices
reflection: Bond breaks get descending two-tone glides. Ambient beds become phase-reactive across the 5-minute arc. Cluster voices gain true stereo panning from centroid X. Biome-tuned LFOs breathe the filter cutoffs.
looking_ahead: Cluster fragmentation sound: when a cluster loses a member, a scatter of brief high pings
Energy-mapped voice brightness: high-energy motes trigger brighter filter cutoffs and shorter decays
Per-biome phase transition variants: the dissolution chord in volcanic should be more dissonant than temperate
Mote-count-aware master mix: scale voice gain inversely with total mote count so complexity doesn't over-saturate

title: Light That Knows the Time
reflection: Water tiles now reflect the phase-tinted sky. Terrain gets phase-aware lighting overlays. Desert heat haze shimmer and storm cloud gradients added. The world feels lit, not just drawn.
looking_ahead: Phase-reactive water level: flood event raises the water line progressively as it plays out
Volcanic ash accumulation overlay: during dissolution, ash tint builds on rocky ground tiles
Rain puddles: small water patches form on flat ground during rain, persist for a few seconds
Tundra aurora: the northern lights effect (currently eclipse-only) should trigger in tundra fog/clear nights

title: The World Learns Its Own Name
reflection: Biomes now narrate in their own voice. First-hearth milestone fires when the first cluster of three forms. Empty-world epitaphs added. Events speak differently per biome, weather-event collisions get combo lines. Drought dries water tile edges.
looking_ahead: Cluster identity persistence: when the same group stays together for 30s+, give them a generated name that appears in narrative ('the RED BASIN cluster endures')
Phase-specific silence epitaphs that vary by what happened — if a plague ran, silence reads differently than if a bloom ended the cycle
Biome-aware aftermath: drought aftermath in desert vs tundra should feel different
A 'near miss' narrative moment: if a cluster almost reaches 6 but dissolves first, acknowledge the loss

title: Atmosphere Made Visible — Particles, Biome Glow, Force Field
reflection: Phase-specific particles: genesis stardust, dissolution ash, silence dust. Bloom pass now tinted per-biome. Cursor force field visualized as a rotating ring that shifts blue-to-red with interaction speed.
looking_ahead: WebGL compositing layer for real HDR tone-mapping — Canvas 2D can only approximate what a proper framebuffer could do
Genesis particle evolution: stardust collects into clusters, mirroring the mote bonding arc
Dissolution ash accumulation on terrain tiles: as ash falls, the ground darkens slightly — the scar of a dying cycle
Phase-reactive vignette color: silence vignette should be dark-blue, dissolution amber-red, not just darker

title: Hardy Shape, Wanderer Lean
reflection: Hardy motes get blocky squared shoulders and silvery-blue shield sparks on hostile terrain. Wanderers lean forward when moving fast. All three temperaments now have unique silhouettes.
looking_ahead: Wanderer trail fade: old trail points shift toward the terrain color, so high-wanderlust motes leave a ghost of where they've been
Social mote cluster merge: when two clusters become one (a new bond forms between cluster members), a brief color-blend flash at the bond site
Hardy elder: old hardy motes develop a second wider shoulder layer — they accumulate visual mass over their lifetime
Low-energy wanderer: a frantic fast-flicker as energy nears zero, vs social motes that dim evenly — different deaths for different characters

title: Three Deaths, Three Characters
reflection: Each temperament now dies differently. Wanderers flicker and stutter, social motes dim and reach outward, hardy motes crack with stress fractures then quake. Dying threshold widened so the arc is visible longer.
looking_ahead: Wanderer trail terrain-fade: old trail breadcrumbs shift toward the underlying ground color, leaving ghostly echoes in the landscape
Social cluster merge flash: when a bond forms between members of two previously separate clusters, a brief color-bloom radiates from the bond midpoint
Hardy elder accumulated mass: motes past age 30 gain a second, wider shoulder layer — visual weight that grows with resilience
Death inheritance: when a mote dies, the nearest living mote briefly flashes the dead mote's color — loss made visible in survivors

title: Five Deaths, Choir Clusters, Volcanic Pulse, Last Survivor
reflection: Each biome now has its own death voice: volcanic cracks and thuds, desert tolls like a distant bell, tundra pings a crystalline ice note with long reverb, lush dissolves into a warm chord, temperate sighs with two voices falling apart. Large clusters grow a 5th harmonic partial that fires stochastically, giving bonded groups choral depth at peak complexity. Volcanic biome gains periodic lava-bubble pops that add industrial texture between notes. The silence phase now holds a long, quietly fading drone when only 1-2 motes remain — the sound of a world breathing its last.
looking_ahead: Desert shimmer accent: very high harmonic pings at low probability, adding the sound of heat haze
Bond formation biome tuning: the ascending fifth chime should use the biome wave type (bell for desert, crystal for tundra)
Chorus on cluster growth event: a special sound when a cluster jumps from 4 to 8+, like a crowd finding its voice
Phase 4-5 reverb swell: as dissolution deepens, very gently increase reverb tail length so everything sounds more distant and ghostly

title: The Cycle Remembers Itself
reflection: Four new emotional beats: event-aware silence epitaphs (plague silence feels different from bloom silence), biome-aware event aftermath (a desert flood vanishes into sand, a tundra bloom freezes in place), a dissolution half-gone line when the world has lost half its peak, and near-miss acknowledgment when a cluster reached 4–5 but never became a great gathering.
looking_ahead: Cluster identity persistence: track named clusters that hold together 30s+, reference them by position in narrative ('the western three endure')
Phase-specific silence variants based on mote count at end: a silent world that lost everyone early vs one where a few elders lingered should feel different
Biome-aware dissolution openers: volcanic dissolution should reference ash, tundra should reference cold closing in
Event combo narrative: if two events run in the same cycle (unlikely but possible), a special acknowledgment line

title: Tundra Aurora, Ash Fall, Biome Clouds
reflection: Tundra biome finally has its night sky: three ribbons of aurora (green, cyan-blue, violet) that undulate as curtains of light during dissolution and silence. Volcanic dissolution is now a slow burial — ground tiles grey toward ash as the cycle ends. Each biome's clouds now have their own character: dusty ochre for desert, icy-blue for tundra, sooty grey for volcanic, humid-bright for lush.
looking_ahead: Phase-reactive water level: flood event raises the shoreline visibly as it plays out — shallow tiles appear at terrain edges
Rain puddles: small water-colour patches on flat ground during rain, a frame or two of sparkle then fade
Tundra aurora interaction: when a large cluster forms under the aurora, the aurora briefly brightens above them
Volcanic sky darkening: as ash accumulates, the sky itself should dim and redden slightly — the world choking on its own output
